title: Fix 'Something Went Wrong' startup crash

requirements.text: Audit and fix the Motoko backend (`backend/main.mo`) for any runtime initialization errors that cause the canister to trap or fail on startup. Ensure all stable variable declarations are valid, all type definitions (Employee with `directManagerId`, Shift with `scheduleStatus`, Timesheet) are consistent and non-conflicting, all pre-seeded data (accounts for 'richaadi0' and 'Adirich') is initialized without errors, and all post-upgrade hooks execute without trapping. The backend must deploy and initialize cleanly without any runtime errors.

quotes: Fix app error it says something went wrong when it started up app

acceptanceCriteria: The backend canister deploys and initializes without trapping or throwing any runtime errors.
All stable variable declarations compile and initialize correctly.
Pre-seeded admin accounts ('richaadi0' and 'Adirich') are present after initialization.
All data type definitions (Employee, Shift, Timesheet) are consistent with no duplicate or conflicting declarations.
The canister survives upgrade cycles without data corruption or initialization traps.

requirements.text: Audit and fix the frontend application startup crash that displays a 'Something went wrong' error screen on initial load. Inspect `frontend/src/App.tsx`, `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, and `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` for any errors that occur during mount — including errors reading from sessionStorage, null/undefined actor access before initialization, broken route component imports, missing lazy-loaded page components, or QueryClient configuration errors. Add error boundaries where needed. The app must load to the login page without any crash screen on first visit.

quotes: Fix app error it says something went wrong when it started up app

acceptanceCriteria: The application loads to the login page without showing any 'Something went wrong' error screen.
No unhandled exceptions are thrown during the React tree mount sequence.
sessionStorage read/write operations are wrapped in try/catch so malformed or missing data does not crash the app.
All route component imports in App.tsx resolve successfully — no missing or incorrectly referenced page components.
The actor initialization in useActor.ts completes without throwing on first load.
React Query's QueryClient is configured without errors and the QueryClientProvider wraps the app correctly.
If an error boundary catches a downstream error, it displays a graceful fallback rather than a blank screen.

requirements.text: Fix all TypeScript compilation errors across the frontend that may be causing the startup crash. Resolve type mismatches on the `directManagerId` field (Employee type), `scheduleStatus` field (Shift type), and all Timesheet-related types across `useQueries.ts`, `EmployeeTimesheetPage.tsx`, `ManagerTimesheetPage.tsx`, `HRTimesheetPage.tsx`, scheduling pages, and employee management pages. Ensure the project builds without TypeScript errors or warnings that would prevent successful deployment.

quotes: Fix app error it says something went wrong when it started up app

acceptanceCriteria: The frontend TypeScript compilation completes with zero errors.
All page components that reference `directManagerId` or `scheduleStatus` fields compile without type errors.
All timesheet page components correctly type the data returned from the `useTimesheets` query.
No implicit `any` types or undefined variable references remain in any page, hook, or context file.
The Vite build process completes successfully and produces a deployable bundle.

constraints: Do not change the immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor file backend/main.mo.
Pre-seeded admin accounts (richaadi0/3vtpxdgf and Adirich/3vtpxdgf) must remain intact and functional after fixes.
nonGoals: Adding new features or pages not already defined in the existing feature set.
Changing the visual theme or redesigning any UI components.
Modifying the backend data model beyond what is required to fix initialization errors.
